result:
Added an `edit` command to the CLI in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L31). `edit <id> [content]` now works in two modes: with content it rewrites the note body while preserving the existing title ([main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L141)); with no content it opens the note file in `$EDITOR` using the current terminal stdio ([main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L209)). I also factored note rendering into a shared formatter so `create` and `edit` produce consistent Markdown ([main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L80), [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L278)).

I added tests covering CLI body replacement, missing-`EDITOR` behavior, and routing through `run()` in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go#L11), and updated the usage/docs in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L298) and [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md#L7).

I could not run `go test` or `gofmt` here because the Go toolchain is not installed in this environment.
